journal_entry.description: Purchase of tea, beverages, and refreshments for client entertainment
journal_entry.rationale: Client entertainment is an operating expense. The 10% tax mentioned is likely PST on prepared food/beverages (not HST-eligible), so it is capitalized into the expense rather than recorded as HST Receivable. Corporate credit card creates a liability.

output_debit_classifier.reason: Client entertainment expense paid by corporate credit card. The 10% tax is non-recoverable (likely PST on prepared beverages/food items which are not HST-eligible in ON), so the full $1,320 is expensed. Debit tuple: 1 asset increase (if prepaid) or 1 expense increase; credit tuple: 1 liability increase. Since this is for immediate client entertainment (not inventory for resale), it's recorded as Meals and Entertainment Expense.
